mnemonics.title: Encoder Input-Output Relationship
mnemonics.content: ENCODER: 2^N INPUTS BECOME N OUTPUTS. TO REMEMBER: ENCODE = COMPRESS (MANY TO FEWER)

mnemonics.title: Binary vs Priority Encoder
mnemonics.content: BINARY ENCODER: ONE INPUT ONLY. PRIORITY ENCODER: MANY INPUTS OKAY. REMEMBER: P FOR PROBLEM-SOLVER (MULTIPLE INPUTS)

mnemonics.title: Encoder vs Decoder
mnemonics.content: ENCODER: MANY TO FEWER (2^N → N). DECODER: FEWER TO MANY (N → 2^N). REMEMBER: E FOR ENCODE-COMPRESS, D FOR DECODE-EXPAND

acronyms.usage: In priority encoders, V=1 means at least one input is active; V=0 means no active inputs
Used in decimal-to-BCD encoders that convert 10 input lines to 4-bit output
In encoders, Y1 (in 4-to-2) is the MSB of the output binary code
In encoders, Y0 (in 4-to-2) is the LSB of the output binary code

visualTips.visualization: Think of a 2-bit binary counter: D0→00, D1→01, D2→10, D3→11. The output binary number equals the decimal index of the active input.
Imagine a priority line where higher-numbered inputs override lower ones. Like senior staff making decisions—highest rank wins.
Encoder: Multiple buttons (2^N) → One binary code (N wires). Decoder: One binary code (N wires) → Multiple lights (2^N). Think: Many-to-few vs Few-to-many.
For Y output bit, draw a line connecting all input indices that have a 1 in that bit position of their binary representation.

analogies.analogy: Like a telephone switchboard—many incoming lines (inputs) get routed to a compressed digital code (output) that represents which line is active.
Like a sports competition with multiple finishers—whichever athlete crosses first (highest priority) gets the gold medal (output code), even if others finish simultaneously.
Think of grouping 3 binary digits like grouping 3 students to represent one octal digit—just as 3 students form one team, 3 bits form one octal digit.
When you press a key, the keyboard encoder converts which key (from 104 keys) into a binary scan code that the computer can process—many keys compressed to 8-bit code.
